tools.description: Add a comment to an existing GitHub issue, pull request, or discussion. Use this to provide feedback, answer questions, or add information to an existing conversation. For creating new items, use create_issue, create_discussion, or create_pull_request instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 1 comment(s) can be added. Target: 37853.

body.description: The comment text in Markdown format. This is the 'body' field - do not use 'comment_body' or other variations. Provide helpful, relevant information that adds value to the conversation.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

item_number.description: The issue, pull request, or discussion number to comment on. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 123 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/123). Can also be a temporary_id (e.g., 'aw_abc123') from a previously created issue in the same workflow run. If omitted, the tool will attempt to resolve the target from the current workflow context (triggering issue, PR, or discussion). If auto-targeting fails (e.g., for schedule or workflow_dispatch triggers), the tool call will fail with an error.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

temporary_id.description: Unique temporary identifier for this comment. Format: 'aw_' followed by 3 to 12 alphanumeric characters (e.g., 'aw_abc1', 'aw_Test123'). Auto-generated if not provided. The temporary ID is returned in the tool response so you can reference this comment later.

tools.name: add_comment

tools.description: Create a review comment on a specific line of code in a pull request. Use this for inline code review feedback, suggestions, or questions about specific code changes. For general PR comments not tied to specific lines, use add_comment instead. When the workflow is configured with `target: "*"`, you must specify `pull_request_number` to indicate which PR to target. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 10 review comment(s) can be created. Comments will be on the RIGHT side of the diff.

body.description: Review comment content in Markdown. Provide specific, actionable feedback about the code at this location.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

line.description: Line number for the comment. For single-line comments, this is the target line. For multi-line comments, this is the ending line.

path.description: File path relative to the repository root (e.g., 'src/auth/login.js'). Must be a file that was changed in the PR.

pull_request_number.description: Pull request number to add the review comment to. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 876 in github.com/owner/repo/pull/876). If omitted, adds the comment to the PR that triggered this workflow. Required when the workflow target is '*' (any PR) — omitting it will cause the comment to fail.

repo.description: Target repository in 'owner/repo' format. If omitted, uses the configured target repository. Must be in the allowed-repos list if specified.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

side.description: Side of the diff to comment on: RIGHT for the new version (additions), LEFT for the old version (deletions). Defaults to RIGHT.

start_line.description: Starting line number for multi-line comments. When set, the comment spans from start_line to line. Omit for single-line comments.

tools.name: create_pull_request_review_comment

tools.description: Submit a pull request review with a status decision. All create_pull_request_review_comment outputs are automatically collected and included as inline comments in this review. Use APPROVE to approve the PR, REQUEST_CHANGES to request changes, or COMMENT for general feedback without a decision. If you don't call this tool, review comments are still submitted as a COMMENT review. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 1 review(s) can be submitted.

body.description: Overall review summary in Markdown. Provide a high-level assessment of the changes. Required for REQUEST_CHANGES; optional for APPROVE and COMMENT.

event.description: Review decision: APPROVE to approve the pull request, REQUEST_CHANGES to formally request changes before merging, or COMMENT for general feedback without a formal decision. Defaults to COMMENT when omitted.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: submit_pull_request_review

tools.description: Push committed changes to a pull request's branch. Use this to add follow-up commits to an existing PR, such as addressing review feedback or fixing issues. Changes must be committed locally before calling this tool. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 3 push(es) can be made.

branch.description: Branch name to push changes from. If omitted, uses the current working branch. Only specify if you need to push from a different branch.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

message.description: Commit message describing the changes. Follow repository commit message conventions (e.g., conventional commits).

pull_request_number.description: Pull request number to push changes to. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 654 in github.com/owner/repo/pull/654). Required when the workflow target is '*' (any PR).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: push_to_pull_request_branch

tools.description: Report that a tool or capability needed to complete the task is not available, or share any information you deem important about missing functionality or limitations. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because the required functionality is missing or access is restricted.

alternatives.description: Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

reason.description: Explanation of why this tool is needed or what information you want to share about the limitation (max 256 characters).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tool.description: Optional: Name or description of the missing tool or capability (max 128 characters). Be specific about what functionality is needed.

tools.name: missing_tool

tools.description: Log a transparency message when no significant actions are needed. Use this to confirm workflow completion and provide visibility when analysis is complete but no changes or outputs are required (e.g., 'No issues found', 'All checks passed'). This ensures the workflow produces human-visible output even when no other actions are taken.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

message.description: Status or completion message to log. Should explain what was analyzed and the outcome (e.g., 'Code review complete - no issues found', 'Analysis complete - all tests passing').

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: noop

tools.description: Report that data or information needed to complete the task is not available. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because required data, context, or information is missing.

alternatives.description: Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).

context.description: Additional context about the missing data or where it should come from (max 256 characters).

data_type.description: Type or description of the missing data or information (max 128 characters). Be specific about what data is needed.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

reason.description: Explanation of why this data is needed to complete the task (max 256 characters).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: missing_data

tools.description: Validate repo-memory files are within configured size limits before the workflow completes. Call this after writing files to memory to check that the total size is within limits. Returns an error if files are too large, with guidance on how to reduce memory size so the memory can be saved successfully.

memory_id.description: Memory identifier to validate. Defaults to 'default' if not specified.

tools.name: push_repo_memory

annotations.title: Get details of GitHub Actions resources (workflows, workflow runs, jobs, and artifacts)

tools.description:
Get details about specific GitHub Actions resources.
Use this tool to get details about individual workflows, workflow runs, jobs, and artifacts by their unique IDs.

resource_id.description:
The unique identifier of the resource. This will vary based on the "method" provided, so ensure you provide the correct ID:
- Provide a workflow ID or workflow file name (e.g. ci.yaml) for 'get_workflow' method.
- Provide a workflow run ID for 'get_workflow_run', 'get_workflow_run_usage', and 'get_workflow_run_logs_url' methods.
- Provide an artifact ID for 'download_workflow_run_artifact' method.
- Provide a job ID for 'get_workflow_job' method.

tools.name: actions_get

annotations.title: List GitHub Actions workflows in a repository

tools.description:
Tools for listing GitHub Actions resources.
Use this tool to list workflows in a repository, or list workflow runs, jobs, and artifacts for a specific workflow or workflow run.

per_page.description: Results per page for pagination (default: 30, max: 100)

resource_id.description:
The unique identifier of the resource. This will vary based on the "method" provided, so ensure you provide the correct ID:
- Do not provide any resource ID for 'list_workflows' method.
- Provide a workflow ID or workflow file name (e.g. ci.yaml) for 'list_workflow_runs' method, or omit to list all workflow runs in the repository.
- Provide a workflow run ID for 'list_workflow_jobs' and 'list_workflow_run_artifacts' methods.

workflow_jobs_filter.description: Filters for workflow jobs. **ONLY** used when method is 'list_workflow_jobs'

filter.description: Filters jobs by their completed_at timestamp

workflow_runs_filter.description: Filters for workflow runs. **ONLY** used when method is 'list_workflow_runs'

actor.description: Filter to a specific GitHub user's workflow runs.

branch.description: Filter workflow runs to a specific Git branch. Use the name of the branch.

event.description: Filter workflow runs to a specific event type

status.description: Filter workflow runs to only runs with a specific status

tools.name: actions_list

tools.description: Get details for a commit from a GitHub repository

include_diff.description: Whether to include file diffs and stats in the response. Default is true.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

sha.description: Commit SHA, branch name, or tag name

tools.name: get_commit

tools.description: Get the contents of a file or directory from a GitHub repository

ref.description: Accepts optional git refs such as `refs/tags/{tag}`, `refs/heads/{branch}` or `refs/pull/{pr_number}/head`

sha.description: Accepts optional commit SHA. If specified, it will be used instead of ref

tools.name: get_file_contents

annotations.title: Get GitHub Actions workflow job logs

tools.description:
Get logs for GitHub Actions workflow jobs.
Use this tool to retrieve logs for a specific job or all failed jobs in a workflow run.
For single job logs, provide job_id. For all failed jobs in a run, provide run_id with failed_only=true.

failed_only.description: When true, gets logs for all failed jobs in the workflow run specified by run_id. Requires run_id to be provided.

job_id.description: The unique identifier of the workflow job. Required when getting logs for a single job.

return_content.description: Returns actual log content instead of URLs

run_id.description: The unique identifier of the workflow run. Required when failed_only is true to get logs for all failed jobs in the run.

tail_lines.description: Number of lines to return from the end of the log

tools.name: get_job_logs

tools.description: Get the latest release in a GitHub repository
tools.name: get_latest_release

tools.description: Get details of the authenticated GitHub user. Use this when a request is about the user's own profile for GitHub. Or when information is missing to build other tool calls.
tools.name: get_me

annotations.title: Get a release by tag name

tools.description: Get a specific release by its tag name in a GitHub repository
tools.name: get_release_by_tag

tools.description: Get details about a specific git tag in a GitHub repository
tools.name: get_tag

tools.description: Get member usernames of a specific team in an organization. Limited to organizations accessible with current credentials

org.description: Organization login (owner) that contains the team.

tools.name: get_team_members

tools.description: Get details of the teams the user is a member of. Limited to organizations accessible with current credentials

user.description: Username to get teams for. If not provided, uses the authenticated user.

tools.name: get_teams

tools.description: List branches in a GitHub repository

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

tools.name: list_branches

tools.description: Get list of commits of a branch in a GitHub repository. Returns at least 30 results per page by default, but can return more if specified using the perPage parameter (up to 100).

author.description: Author username or email address to filter commits by

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

sha.description: Commit SHA, branch or tag name to list commits of. If not provided, uses the default branch of the repository. If a commit SHA is provided, will list commits up to that SHA.

tools.name: list_commits

tools.description: List pull requests in a GitHub repository. If the user specifies an author, then DO NOT use this tool and use the search_pull_requests tool instead.

head.description: Filter by head user/org and branch

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

tools.name: list_pull_requests

tools.description: List releases in a GitHub repository

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

tools.name: list_releases

tools.description: List git tags in a GitHub repository

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

tools.name: list_tags

annotations.title: Get details for a single pull request

tools.description: Get information on a specific pull request in GitHub repository.

method.description:
Action to specify what pull request data needs to be retrieved from GitHub. 
Possible options: 
 1. get - Get details of a specific pull request.
 2. get_diff - Get the diff of a pull request.
 3. get_status - Get combined commit status of a head commit in a pull request.
 4. get_files - Get the list of files changed in a pull request. Use with pagination parameters to control the number of results returned.
 5. get_review_comments - Get review threads on a pull request. Each thread contains logically grouped review comments made on the same code location during pull request reviews. Returns threads with metadata (isResolved, isOutdated, isCollapsed) and their associated comments. Use cursor-based pagination (perPage, after) to control results.
 6. get_reviews - Get the reviews on a pull request. When asked for review comments, use get_review_comments method.
 7. get_comments - Get comments on a pull request. Use this if user doesn't specifically want review comments. Use with pagination parameters to control the number of results returned.
 8. get_check_runs - Get check runs for the head commit of a pull request. Check runs are the individual CI/CD jobs and checks that run on the PR.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

tools.name: pull_request_read

tools.description: Fast and precise code search across ALL GitHub repositories using GitHub's native search engine. Best for finding exact symbols, functions, classes, or specific code patterns.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

query.description: Search query using GitHub's powerful code search syntax. Examples: 'content:Skill language:Java org:github', 'NOT is:archived language:Python OR language:go', 'repo:github/github-mcp-server'. Supports exact matching, language filters, path filters, and more.

tools.name: search_code

tools.description: Search for pull requests in GitHub repositories using issues search syntax already scoped to is:pr

owner.description: Optional repository owner. If provided with repo, only pull requests for this repository are listed.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

query.description: Search query using GitHub pull request search syntax

repo.description: Optional repository name. If provided with owner, only pull requests for this repository are listed.

sort.description: Sort field by number of matches of categories, defaults to best match

tools.name: search_pull_requests

tools.description: Find GitHub repositories by name, description, readme, topics, or other metadata. Perfect for discovering projects, finding examples, or locating specific repositories across GitHub.

minimal_output.description: Return minimal repository information (default: true). When false, returns full GitHub API repository objects.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

query.description: Repository search query. Examples: 'machine learning in:name stars:>1000 language:python', 'topic:react', 'user:facebook'. Supports advanced search syntax for precise filtering.

sort.description: Sort repositories by field, defaults to best match

tools.name: search_repositories

🔴 **Tool Issue** — `review/arm-cognitiveservices-node.api.md:258`

`_UsageListResult` is used as the return type of `AccountsOperations.listUsages` but is not exported from the entry point `index.d.ts` (API Extractor warning: `ae-forgotten-export`).

The underscore prefix indicates this type is internal, but since it appears in the public API surface, it must be exported with a proper public name.

**Fix:** Export `UsageListResult` (without the `_` prefix) from `src/index.ts`, or report this issue in the [generation tool repository](https://github.com/Azure/autorest.typescript/issues) so the tool can be fixed to generate the correct export.

🔴 **Tool Issue** — `review/arm-cognitiveservices-node.api.md:2484`

The underscore prefix indicates this type is internal, but since it appears in the public API surface, it must be exported with a proper public name.

**Fix:** Export `OutboundRuleListResult` (without the `_` prefix) from `src/index.ts`, or report this issue in the [generation tool repository](https://github.com/Azure/autorest.typescript/issues) so the tool can be fixed to generate the correct export.
